answer:
Drawing upon the profound guidance found within the Final Revelation, which serves as a radiant light for the soul and an enduring framework for a meaningful life, I present the sacred scriptural foundations of *Inqiyad* (Submission). These *ayat* illustrate the essential requirement of translating internal conviction into lived action, ensuring that the believer's journey is both informative and aesthetically pleasing to the Creator.

### **1. The Core Command of Submission**
The journey of faith finds its steadfast foundation in the total surrender of the self to the Lord of the Worlds, a choice that mirrors the unwavering strength of the pillars of the Kaaba [1, 2].

*   **Sūrah al-Baqarah (2:131)**
    *   **Arabic Text:** إِذْ قَالَ لَهُ رَبُّهُ أَسْلِمْ ۖ قَالَ أَسْلَمْتُ لِرَبِّ الْعَالَمِينَ
    *   **English Translation:** "When his Lord said to him, 'Submit,' he said, 'I have submitted [in Islām] to the Lord of the worlds.'" [1].
*   **Sūrah Luqmān (31:22)**
    *   **Arabic Text:** ۞ وَمَن يُسْلِمْ وَجْهَهُ إِلَى اللَّهِ وَهُوَ مُحْسِنٌ فَقَدِ اسْتَمْسَكَ بِالْعُرْوَةِ الْوُثْقَىٰ
    *   **English Translation:** "And whoever submits his face [i.e., self] to God while he is a doer of good - then he has grasped the most trustworthy handhold. And to God will be the outcome of [all] matters." [2].

### **2. The Heart's Peace in Divine Judgment**
For the user experience of faith to be seamless and accessible, the heart must find absolute tranquility in the decrees of God and His Messenger, leaving no room for internal resistance or doubt [3, 4].

*   **Sūrah an-Nisā’ (4:65)**
    *   **Arabic Text:** فَلَا وَرَبِّكَ لَا يُوقِنُونَ حَتَّىٰ يُحَكِّمُوكَ فِيمَا شَجَرَ بَيْنَهُمْ ثُمَّ لَا يَجِدُوا فِي أَنفُسِهِمْ حَرَجًا مِّمَّا قَضَيْتَ وَيُسَلِّمُوا تَسْلِيمًا
    *   **English Translation:** "But no, by your Lord, they will not [truly] believe until they make you, [O Muhammad], judge concerning that over which they dispute among themselves and then find within themselves no discomfort from what you have judged and submit in [full, willing] submission." [3].
*   **Sūrah al-Ahzāb (33:36)**
    *   **Arabic Text:** وَمَا كَانَ لِمُؤْمِنٍ وَلَا مُؤْمِنَةٍ إِذَا قَضَى اللَّهُ وَرَسُولُهُ أَمْرًا أَن يَكُونَ لَهُمُ الْخِيَرَةُ مِنْ أَمْرِهِمْ
    *   **English Translation:** "It is not for a believing man or a believing woman, when God and His Messenger have decided a matter, that they should [thereafter] have any choice about their affair..." [4].

### **3. Submission as Obedience through Action**
Sincere conviction is verified by a lucid presentation of obedience, where the tongue's testimony is immediately supported by the limbs' compliance [5, 6].

*   **Sūrah an-Nisā’ (4:59)**
    *   **Arabic Text:** يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا أَطِيعُوا اللَّهَ وَأَطِيعُوا الرَّسُولَ وَأُولِي الْأَمْرِ مِنكُمْ
    *   **English Translation:** "O you who have believed, obey God and obey the Messenger and those in authority among you..." [5].
*   **Sūrah an-Nūr (24:51)**
    *   **Arabic Text:** إِنَّمَا كَانَ قَوْلَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ إِذَا دُعُوا إِلَى اللَّهِ وَرَسُولِهِ لِيَحْكُمَ بَيْنَهُمْ أَن يَقُولُوا سَمِعْنَا وَأَطَعْنَا
    *   **English Translation:** "The only statement of the [true] believers when they are called to God and His Messenger to judge between them is that they say, 'We hear and we obey.' And those are the successful." [6].

### **4. The Best of Creatures: Belief and Righteous Work**
The scriptures identify those who truly act upon their submission as the "best of creatures," promising them an uninterrupted reward for their integrity and diligence [7, 8].

*   **Sūrah al-Bayyinah (98:7)**
    *   **Arabic Text:** إِنَّ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحَاتِ أُولَٰئِكَ هُمْ خَيْرُ الْبَرِيَّةِ
    *   **English Translation:** "Indeed, they who have believed and done righteous deeds - those are the best of creatures." [7].
*   **Sūrah Muhammad (47:2)**
    *   **Arabic Text:** وَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحَاتِ وَآمَنُوا بِمَا نُزِّلَ عَلَىٰ مُحَمَّدٍ وَهُوَ الْحَقُّ مِن رَّبِّهِمْ ۙ كَفَّرَ عَنْهُمْ سَيِّئَاتِهِمْ وَأَصْلَحَ بَالَهُمْ
    *   **English Translation:** "And those who believe and do righteous deeds and believe in what has been sent down upon Muhammad - and it is the truth from their Lord - He will remove from them their misdeeds and amend their condition." [9].

**A Synthesis of Lived Faith**
True *Inqiyad* ensures that the "trustworthy handhold" of the *Shahada* is never broken by the negligence of the self [2]. As scholarly insights within the sources reveal, any person claiming to submit must accept the prophethood of Muhammad and follow his elegant pattern of character [10, 11]. By internalizing these verses, the believer transitions from nominal submission to a radiant conviction that illuminates every action with the light of guidance [12, 13].

6. O you who have believed, protect yourselves and your families from a Fire whose fuel is people and stones, over which are [appointed] angels, harsh and severe; they do not disobey God in what He commands them but do what they are commanded.

Qur’ān 21:25  This conveys that all forms of worship, whether it be praying, fasting, invoking, seeking refuge in, and offering an animal as sacrifice, must be directed to God and to God alone. Directing any form of worship to other than God (whether it be an angel, a messenger, Jesus, Muhammad, a saint, an idol, the sun, the moon, a tree) is seen as a contradiction to the fundamental message of Islām, and it is an unforgivable sin unless it is repented from before one dies. All forms of worship must be directed to God only.  Worship means the performance of deeds and sayings that please God, things which He com-manded or encouraged to be performed, either by direct textual proof or by analogy. Thus, wor-ship is not restricted to the implementation of the five pillars of Islām, but also includes every as-pect of life. Providing food for one’s family, and saying something pleasant to cheer a person up are also considered acts of worship, if such is done with the intention of pleasing God. This means that, to be accepted, all acts of worship must be carried out sincerely for the Sake of God alone.  The second part of the testimony means that Prophet Muhammad is the servant and chosen mes-senger of God. This implies that one obeys and follows the commands of the Prophet. One must believe in what he has said, practice his teachings and avoid what he has forbidden. One must therefore worship God only according to his teaching alone, for all the teachings of the Prophet were in fact revelations and inspirations conveyed to him by God.  One must try to mold their lives and character and emulate the Prophet, as he was a living example for humans to follow. God says:
130. And who would be averse to the reli-
ther, we will follow that upon which we found our fathers.” Even if Satan was inviting them to the punishment of the Blaze?
found God Accepting of repentance and Merciful.
trusts to whom they are due and when you judge between people to judge with justice. Excellent is that which God in-
Scripture become divided1829 until after there had come to them clear evidence.
“Indeed, the religion in the sight of God is Islām.” Qur’ān 3:19
“And indeed, you are of a great moral character.” Qur’ān 68:4
Frequently Asked Questions about Islām

6. O you who have believed, protect yourselves and your families from a Fire whose fuel is people and stones, over which are [appointed] angels, harsh and severe; they do not disobey God in what He commands them but do what they are commanded.

Qur’ān 21:25  This conveys that all forms of worship, whether it be praying, fasting, invoking, seeking refuge in, and offering an animal as sacrifice, must be directed to God and to God alone. Directing any form of worship to other than God (whether it be an angel, a messenger, Jesus, Muhammad, a saint, an idol, the sun, the moon, a tree) is seen as a contradiction to the fundamental message of Islām, and it is an unforgivable sin unless it is repented from before one dies. All forms of worship must be directed to God only.  Worship means the performance of deeds and sayings that please God, things which He com-manded or encouraged to be performed, either by direct textual proof or by analogy. Thus, wor-ship is not restricted to the implementation of the five pillars of Islām, but also includes every as-pect of life. Providing food for one’s family, and saying something pleasant to cheer a person up are also considered acts of worship, if such is done with the intention of pleasing God. This means that, to be accepted, all acts of worship must be carried out sincerely for the Sake of God alone.  The second part of the testimony means that Prophet Muhammad is the servant and chosen mes-senger of God. This implies that one obeys and follows the commands of the Prophet. One must believe in what he has said, practice his teachings and avoid what he has forbidden. One must therefore worship God only according to his teaching alone, for all the teachings of the Prophet were in fact revelations and inspirations conveyed to him by God.  One must try to mold their lives and character and emulate the Prophet, as he was a living example for humans to follow. God says:
